gene_description: NBPF3 (NACHT, LRR and PYD domains-containing protein 3) — variant located in intergenic region affecting ALPL gene function; ALPL is primary enzyme responsible for catabolism and clearance of vitamin B6 in plasma

source.title: Genome-wide association study of vitamin B6, vitamin B12, folate, and homocysteine blood concentrations
source.finding: rs4654748 C allele associated with 1.38 ng/mL lower plasma vitamin B6 concentrations (p<0.001); variant influences ALPL-mediated B6 catabolism

CC.effect: Homozygous C carriers have significantly lower plasma pyridoxal-5'-phosphate (PLP, active B6 form) concentrations. Variant associated with enhanced ALPL enzyme activity and accelerated B6 metabolism. Requires higher dietary intake to maintain adequate B6 status.
CC.recommendation: Increase dietary vitamin B6 intake to 1.5-2.0 mg daily (above standard RDA of 1.3-1.7 mg). Monitor for B6 deficiency symptoms (peripheral neuropathy, anemia, dermatitis). Consider B6 supplementation (50-100 mg daily) if dietary intake insufficient.

CT.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate plasma B6 levels, between homozygous groups. Modest increase in ALPL-mediated catabolism compared to TT wild-type.
CT.recommendation: Standard vitamin B6 RDA typically adequate (1.3-1.7 mg daily for adults). Monitor dietary intake, particularly if consuming alcohol or medications that increase B6 requirements. Supplementation if deficiency signs develop.

TC.effect: Heterozygotes show intermediate plasma B6 levels, between homozygous groups. Modest increase in ALPL-mediated catabolism compared to TT wild-type.
TC.recommendation: Standard vitamin B6 RDA typically adequate (1.3-1.7 mg daily for adults). Monitor dietary intake, particularly if consuming alcohol or medications that increase B6 requirements. Supplementation if deficiency signs develop.

TT.effect: Wild-type homozygotes have normal ALPL activity and vitamin B6 catabolism. Normal plasma B6 levels with standard dietary intake.
TT.recommendation: Standard dietary vitamin B6 recommendations adequate (1.3-1.7 mg daily for adults). Maintain varied diet including poultry, fish, potatoes, chickpeas, and whole grains.
